name: Image
summary: Theme-aware `` wrapper with sizing and object-fit ergonomics for static raster/vector images; use it for content imagery, and `Avatar` for circular user pictures or `Video` for playable media.

props.name: alt
props.description: Required alt text. Use empty string for decorative images.

props.name: width
props.description:
CSS width (number ⇒ px). When BOTH `width` and `height` are numeric they are
ALSO emitted as the native HTML width/height attributes, so the browser can
reserve layout space and avoid CLS. A string value (e.g. '50%', '20rem') is
CSS-only and does NOT set the intrinsic-size attributes.

props.name: height
props.description: CSS height (number ⇒ px). See ImageProps.width re: native attrs.

props.name: aspectRatio
props.description:
CSS aspect-ratio. A number is the literal width/height ratio — pass a real
ratio like `16 / 9` or `4 / 3` (NOT `16`, which is 16:1). Strings pass
through verbatim (e.g. '16 / 9').

props.name: radius
props.description:
Corner radius. Number ⇒ `theme.radius(n)` (scales with density); string ⇒
verbatim CSS. Rounds the image itself — no wrapper needed. (A parent with
`overflow: hidden` is still the way to CLIP overflowing/child content.)

props.name: priority
props.description:
Mark as a high-priority (above-the-fold / LCP) image: sets `loading='eager'`
and `fetchPriority='high'`. An explicit `loading`/`fetchPriority` still wins.
Default false (images load lazy + decode async).

props.name: fallback
props.description: Opt-in node rendered if the image fails to load (onError). When unset, a failed image behaves exactly like a native `` — no wrapper, no state. The caller sizes the fallback node.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

bestPractices: Always set alt text; use empty alt for decorative images.
Avoid layout shift: a NUMERIC width AND height emit the native size attributes so the browser reserves space, or set `aspectRatio` (a number is the literal ratio — pass `16 / 9`, not `16`).
Choose fit (`cover`/`contain`) and objectPosition deliberately to control cropping/focus.
Use srcSet/sizes (native `` passthrough) for responsive sources — valet has no build-step optimizer, so caller-provided sources are the responsive story.
Lazy-loads + async-decodes by default; set `priority` for an above-the-fold/LCP hero (eager + fetchPriority=high). An explicit loading/fetchPriority still wins.
Round corners with the `radius` prop (number → theme.radius(n), scales with density); reach for a parent `overflow: hidden` wrapper only to clip overflowing/child content.
Pass `fallback` to degrade a broken image gracefully (rendered onError); without it a failed load behaves like a native .
Avoid background images for content; prefer `<Image>` so a11y tools and AI proxies can interpret it.
